verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes the inducible cytosolic Hsp70 chaperone, canonically intracellular. However, there is substantial published evidence that Hsp70 (HSP70-1/HSPA1A) is displayed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, particularly on tumor cells and under heat-stress conditions, without a classical TM domain or GPI anchor — likely via direct interaction with membrane lipids (phosphatidylserine) or membrane-associated partners. This cell-surface pool has been exploited therapeutically: NK-cell-based and antibody-based approaches target surface Hsp70 on cancer cells. The surface display is stress- and transformation-induced, fitting 'cell_state_induced'. It does not occur constitutively under baseline conditions in normal somatic cells, making 'yes' too strong. Pre-no checklist: cancer/oncogenic ecto-pool is well-documented for this specific protein; cell-death surface display also reported; no germline restriction; not a lysosomal TM protein; no stable TM-partner tethering known — the lipid-interaction mechanism is the proposed anchor.
